title: centaurea rutifolia
intro: Centaurea rutifolia, also known as Mountain Bluet or Mountain Cornflower, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Its leaves and flowers have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce pain. The plant is also known for its health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestion. Centaurea rutifolia can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural health practitioners.
